description_long: This object is a detailed model of a spiny crustacean, likely a species of lobster or squat lobster, with a robust, segmented body and a pronounced curve in its abdomen. The cephalothorax is covered in spiny projections and features two prominent claws, one larger than the other, as well as several walking legs. Long, thin antennae extend from the head, and the eyes are set on short stalks, giving it a distinctive appearance. The surface is rough and textured, with a mottled coloration of reddish-brown, orange, and hints of purple, mimicking the natural exoskeleton of marine crustaceans. The underside is lighter and less spiny, while the dorsal side is heavily armored and covered in bristles.
description: A spiny, reddish-brown crustacean with segmented body, large claws, and long antennae, featuring a rough, textured exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the pronounced curve of the abdomen, spiny cephalothorax, and both claws clearly visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the face, long antennae, eye stalks, and the symmetry of the claws and legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail's curve, spiny back, and the arrangement of walking legs.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the segmentation of the body, the spread of the claws, and the dorsal spines.
